sentences.sentence: There is a poem, written around 598 AD, which describes hunting a mystery animal called a llewyn.
sentences.translation: 有一首大约写于公元598年的诗，描述了猎捕一种名叫 llewyn 的神秘动物。

structure.text: There is a poem
written around 598 AD
which describes hunting a mystery animal called a llewyn
called a llewyn

difficult_points: There is 引出新信息，后面的 poem 是全句讨论对象。
written around 598 AD 是过去分词短语，补充诗的写作时间。
which 引导定语从句，说明这首诗描述的内容。

sentences.sentence: But what was it?
sentences.translation: 但它究竟是什么呢？

structure.text: what
was
it

difficult_points: But 表示转折，引出对 llewyn 身份的疑问。
it 回指上一句中的 a mystery animal called a llewyn。

sentences.sentence: Nothing seemed to fit, until 2006, when an animal bone, dating from around the same period, was found in the Kinsey Cave in northern England.
sentences.translation: 似乎没有任何解释说得通，直到2006年，一块年代大约相同的动物骨头在英格兰北部的金西洞穴被发现。

structure.text: Nothing seemed to fit
until 2006
when an animal bone, dating from around the same period, was found in the Kinsey Cave in northern England
dating from around the same period
was found in the Kinsey Cave in northern England

difficult_points: Nothing seemed to fit 表示之前没有任何解释能够匹配这个神秘动物。
until 2006 引出转折性的时间节点。
when 引导非限制性定语从句，补充2006年发生的发现。
dating from around the same period 修饰 an animal bone，说明骨头年代相近。

sentences.sentence: Until this discovery, the lynx - a large spotted cat with tasselled ears - was presumed to have died out in Britain at least 6,000 years ago, before the inhabitants of these islands took up farming.
sentences.translation: 在这一发现之前，人们认为猞猁——一种长着簇毛耳朵的大型斑点猫科动物——至少在6000年前就已在英国灭绝，早于这些岛屿上的居民开始从事农耕。

structure.text: Until this discovery
the lynx
a large spotted cat with tasselled ears
was presumed to have died out in Britain at least 6,000 years ago
before the inhabitants of these islands took up farming

difficult_points: 破折号中的 a large spotted cat with tasselled ears 是对 the lynx 的插入解释。
was presumed to have died out 是被动表达，强调这是过去的推定。
before 从句说明被认为灭绝的时间早于当地居民开始农业。

sentences.sentence: But the 2006 find, together with three others in Yorkshire and Scotland, is compelling evidence that the lynx and the mysterious llewyn were in fact one and the same animal.
sentences.translation: 但2006年的发现，连同约克郡和苏格兰的另外三项发现，构成了有力证据，表明猞猁和神秘的 llewyn 实际上是同一种动物。

structure.text: the 2006 find, together with three others in Yorkshire and Scotland
together with three others in Yorkshire and Scotland
is compelling evidence
that the lynx and the mysterious llewyn were in fact one and the same animal

difficult_points: But 表示新发现推翻了上一句所说的旧推定。
together with three others 是插入补充，不改变主语核心 the 2006 find。
that 从句解释 evidence 的具体内容。
one and the same animal 强调两种名称指向同一种动物。

sentences.sentence: If this is so, it would bring forward the tassel-eared cat's estimated extinction date by roughly 5,000 years.
sentences.translation: 如果情况确实如此，这会把这种簇耳猫科动物的估计灭绝日期向后推进大约5000年。

structure.text: If this is so
it
would bring forward the tassel-eared cat's estimated extinction date by roughly 5,000 years

difficult_points: If this is so 回指上一句“猞猁与 llewyn 是同一种动物”的判断。
bring forward 在这里表示把估计灭绝日期向较近的年代移动。
by roughly 5,000 years 表示调整幅度，而不是具体年份。

expressions.meaning_zh: 提前；把日期向前或向近处调整

sentences.sentence: However, this is not quite the last glimpse of the animal in British culture.
sentences.translation: 然而，这还并不完全是这种动物在英国文化中最后一次露面。

structure.text: this
is not quite the last glimpse of the animal in British culture
of the animal in British culture

difficult_points: However 表示转向另一个文化证据。
not quite 表示“不完全是”，语气比直接否定更委婉。
the last glimpse 指文化记录中最后一次可见的痕迹。

sentences.sentence: A 9th-century stone cross from the Isle of Eigg shows, alongside the deer, boar and aurochs pursued by a mounted hunter, a speckled cat with tasselled ears.
sentences.translation: 来自艾格岛的一座9世纪石十字架上，在被骑马猎人追逐的鹿、野猪和原牛旁边，刻有一只带簇毛耳朵的斑点猫科动物。

structure.text: A 9th-century stone cross from the Isle of Eigg
shows
alongside the deer, boar and aurochs pursued by a mounted hunter
pursued by a mounted hunter
a speckled cat with tasselled ears

difficult_points: shows 的宾语是 a speckled cat with tasselled ears，中间的 alongside 短语是插入位置说明。
pursued by a mounted hunter 是过去分词短语，修饰 deer, boar and aurochs。
with tasselled ears 是识别猞猁的重要特征。

sentences.sentence: Were it not for the animal's backside having worn away with time, we could have been certain, as the lynx's stubby tail is unmistakable.
sentences.translation: 要不是这只动物的后半身随着时间磨损掉了，我们本可以确定它就是猞猁，因为猞猁短粗的尾巴非常容易辨认。

structure.text: Were it not for the animal's backside having worn away with time
we
could have been certain
as the lynx's stubby tail is unmistakable

difficult_points: Were it not for... 是省略 if 的倒装虚拟条件句，意思是“要不是……”。
having worn away with time 是动名词完成式，说明后半身已经随时间磨损。
could have been certain 表示本可以确定但现实中不能完全确定。
as 引导原因状语从句，解释为什么尾巴能帮助确认。

sentences.sentence: But even without this key feature, it's hard to see what else the creature could have been.
sentences.translation: 但即使没有这一关键特征，也很难看出这种生物还可能是什么别的动物。

structure.text: even without this key feature
it's hard to see
what else the creature could have been

difficult_points: even without this key feature 表示即使缺少尾巴这个关键证据。
it's hard to see 是形式主语结构，真正内容是后面的 what 从句。
could have been 表示对过去身份的推测。

sentences.sentence: The lynx is now becoming the totemic animal of a movement that is transforming British environmentalism: rewilding.
sentences.translation: 猞猁如今正成为一场正在改变英国环保主义运动的图腾动物：这场运动就是再野化。

structure.text: The lynx
is now becoming the totemic animal of a movement
that is transforming British environmentalism
rewilding

difficult_points: is now becoming 使用现在进行时，强调猞猁象征意义正在形成。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 a movement。
冒号后的 rewilding 点明这场运动的名称。

expressions.meaning_zh: 图腾式动物；象征性动物

sentences.sentence: Rewilding means the mass restoration of damaged ecosystems.
sentences.translation: 再野化意味着对受损生态系统进行大规模恢复。

structure.text: Rewilding
means the mass restoration of damaged ecosystems
of damaged ecosystems

difficult_points: 本句给出 rewilding 的核心定义。
mass restoration 强调恢复规模很大，不是局部修补。
damaged ecosystems 是恢复的对象。

sentences.sentence: It involves letting trees return to places that have been denuded, allowing parts of the seabed to recover from trawling and dredging, permitting rivers to flow freely again.
sentences.translation: 它包括让树木重新回到已经被剥光植被的地方，让部分海床从拖网捕捞和疏浚中恢复，并允许河流再次自由流动。

structure.text: It
involves letting trees return to places that have been denuded
that have been denuded
allowing parts of the seabed to recover from trawling and dredging
permitting rivers to flow freely again

difficult_points: involves 后接三个并列的动名词结构：letting、allowing、permitting。
that have been denuded 修饰 places，表示这些地方已被剥夺植被。
to recover from trawling and dredging 说明海床需要从人类活动造成的破坏中恢复。
to flow freely again 表示恢复河流自然流动。

sentences.sentence: Above all, it means bringing back missing species.
sentences.translation: 最重要的是，它意味着让消失的物种回归。

structure.text: Above all
it
means bringing back missing species

difficult_points: Above all 强调在多项措施中最重要的一点。
bringing back 是动名词短语，作 means 的宾语。
missing species 指当地生态系统中已经消失的物种。

sentences.sentence: One of the most striking findings of modern ecology is that ecosystems without large predators behave in completely different ways from those that retain them.
sentences.translation: 现代生态学最引人注目的发现之一是，没有大型捕食者的生态系统与保留大型捕食者的生态系统运作方式完全不同。

structure.text: One of the most striking findings of modern ecology
is
that ecosystems without large predators behave in completely different ways from those that retain them
that retain them

difficult_points: One of... 作主语，谓语用单数 is。
that 引导表语从句，说明这个发现的具体内容。
those 指代 ecosystems，that retain them 修饰 those。
them 指 large predators，而不是 ecosystems。

expressions.meaning_zh: 以完全不同的方式运作

sentences.sentence: Some of them drive dynamic processes that resonate through the whole food chain, creating niches for hundreds of species that might otherwise struggle to survive.
sentences.translation: 其中一些大型捕食者会推动动态过程，这些过程影响整个食物链，并为数百种原本可能难以生存的物种创造生态位。

structure.text: Some of them
drive dynamic processes
that resonate through the whole food chain
creating niches for hundreds of species
that might otherwise struggle to survive

difficult_points: Some of them 指前文的大型捕食者中的一部分。
that resonate through the whole food chain 修饰 dynamic processes，说明影响会贯穿食物链。
creating niches 是现在分词结果状语，说明这些过程创造生态位。
otherwise 表示如果没有这些生态位，许多物种可能难以生存。

sentences.sentence: The killers turn out to be bringers of life.
sentences.translation: 这些杀手结果却成了生命的带来者。

structure.text: The killers
turn out to be bringers of life
bringers of life

difficult_points: The killers 指大型捕食者，带有与后面 bringers of life 的反差。
turn out to be 表示结果证明是。
bringers of life 是比喻性表达，强调捕食者能促进生态多样性。

sentences.sentence: Such findings present a big challenge to British conservation, which has often selected arbitrary assemblages of plants and animals and sought, at great effort and expense, to prevent them from changing.
sentences.translation: 这些发现给英国的自然保护工作提出了巨大挑战，因为后者常常选择任意组合的动植物，并不惜巨大努力和成本试图阻止它们发生变化。

structure.text: Such findings
present a big challenge to British conservation
which has often selected arbitrary assemblages of plants and animals and sought
at great effort and expense
to prevent them from changing

difficult_points: Such findings 回指上一段关于大型捕食者生态作用的发现。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 British conservation。
selected 和 sought 是 which 从句中的并列谓语。
them 指 arbitrary assemblages of plants and animals。

sentences.sentence: It has tried to preserve the living world as if it were a jar of pickles, letting nothing in and nothing out, keeping nature in a state of arrested development.
sentences.translation: 它试图像保存一罐腌菜那样保存生物世界：什么也不让进，什么也不让出，让自然停留在发展停滞的状态。

structure.text: It
has tried to preserve the living world
as if it were a jar of pickles
letting nothing in and nothing out
keeping nature in a state of arrested development

difficult_points: It 指 British conservation，即英国传统保护思路。
as if it were 使用虚拟语气，把生物世界比作一罐腌菜。
letting 和 keeping 两个现在分词短语说明这种保护方式的具体表现。
arrested development 表示发展被阻滞，并非正常稳定。

sentences.sentence: But ecosystems are not merely collections of species; they are also the dynamic and ever-shifting relationships between them.
sentences.translation: 但生态系统并不仅仅是物种的集合；它们也是这些物种之间动态且不断变化的关系。

structure.text: ecosystems
are not merely collections of species
they
are also the dynamic and ever-shifting relationships between them

difficult_points: But 引出作者对传统保护观念的反驳。
not merely... also... 强调生态系统不仅是物种列表，更是关系网络。
them 指前半句的 species。
ever-shifting 表示这些关系持续变化。

sentences.sentence: And this dynamism often depends on large predators.
sentences.translation: 而这种动态性往往依赖大型捕食者。

structure.text: this dynamism
often depends on large predators

difficult_points: And 承接上一句，进一步说明生态系统动态关系的来源。
this dynamism 指物种间动态、不断变化的关系。
depends on 表示大型捕食者是维持这种动态性的关键条件。

sentences.sentence: At sea the potential is even greater: by protecting large areas from commercial fishing, we could once more see what 18th-century literature describes: vast shoals of fish being chased by fin and sperm whales, within sight of the English shore.
sentences.translation: 在海洋中，这种潜力甚至更大：通过保护大片海域免受商业捕鱼影响，我们或许能再次看到18世纪文学中描述的景象：在英国海岸视线范围内，大群鱼被长须鲸和抹香鲸追逐。

structure.text: At sea the potential is even greater
by protecting large areas from commercial fishing
we could once more see what 18th-century literature describes
what 18th-century literature describes
vast shoals of fish being chased by fin and sperm whales

difficult_points: 第一个冒号引出海洋再野化潜力的具体说明。
by protecting... 表示实现这种景象的手段。
what 18th-century literature describes 是 see 的宾语，后一个冒号具体列出这种景象。
being chased by fin and sperm whales 是现在分词被动结构，修饰 vast shoals of fish。

sentences.sentence: This policy would also greatly boost catches in the surrounding seas; the fishing industry's insistence on scouring every inch of seabed, leaving no breeding reserves, could not be more damaging to its own interests.
sentences.translation: 这项政策也会大大增加周边海域的渔获量；渔业坚持搜刮每一寸海床、不留下任何繁殖保护区，这对其自身利益的损害再大不过。

structure.text: This policy would also greatly boost catches in the surrounding seas
the fishing industry's insistence on scouring every inch of seabed, leaving no breeding reserves
on scouring every inch of seabed
leaving no breeding reserves
could not be more damaging to its own interests

difficult_points: 分号连接两个紧密相关的分句，后半句解释商业捕鱼的反效果。
insistence on 后接动名词 scouring，表示持续坚持某种做法。
leaving no breeding reserves 是结果性分词短语。
could not be more damaging 是最高程度表达，意思是“极其有害”。

sentences.sentence: Rewilding is a rare example of an environmental movement in which campaigners articulate what they are for rather than only what they are against.
sentences.translation: 再野化是环保运动中少见的例子：其倡导者会清楚表达他们支持什么，而不只是表达他们反对什么。

structure.text: Rewilding
is a rare example of an environmental movement
in which campaigners articulate what they are for rather than only what they are against
what they are for
what they are against

difficult_points: in which 引导定语从句，修饰 an environmental movement。
articulate 表示清楚表达，而不只是含糊提及。
what they are for 与 what they are against 形成对比。
rather than only 强调再野化不仅仅以反对为核心。

expressions.meaning_zh: 清楚表达他们支持什么

sentences.sentence: One of the reasons why the enthusiasm for rewilding is spreading so quickly in Britain is that it helps to create a more inspiring vision than the green movement's usual promise of 'Follow us and the world will be slightly less awful than it would otherwise have been.'
sentences.translation: 再野化热情在英国迅速扩散的原因之一，是它有助于创造一种比绿色运动通常承诺更鼓舞人心的愿景；后者常说的是：“跟随我们，世界会比原本可能的样子稍微少糟一点。”

structure.text: One of the reasons why the enthusiasm for rewilding is spreading so quickly in Britain
why the enthusiasm for rewilding is spreading so quickly in Britain
is
that it helps to create a more inspiring vision than the green movement's usual promise
'Follow us and the world will be slightly less awful than it would otherwise have been.'

difficult_points: One of the reasons 作主语，why 从句修饰 reasons。
that 引导表语从句，说明原因的具体内容。
than the green movement's usual promise 引出比较对象。
引号中的句子以讽刺方式概括传统环保运动较消极的愿景。

sentences.sentence: The lynx presents no threat to human beings: there is no known instance of one preying on people.
sentences.translation: 猞猁对人类不构成威胁：没有已知的猞猁捕食人的例子。

structure.text: The lynx
presents no threat to human beings
there is no known instance of one preying on people
of one preying on people

difficult_points: 冒号后的分句解释为什么猞猁对人类不构成威胁。
one 指一只猞猁，而不是泛指人。
preying on people 是动名词短语，说明 instance 的内容。

sentences.sentence: It is a specialist predator of roe deer, a species that has exploded in Britain in recent decades, holding back, by intensive browsing, attempts to re-establish forests.
sentences.translation: 它是专门捕食狍鹿的捕食者，而狍鹿这一物种近几十年来在英国数量激增，并因密集啃食而阻碍了重建森林的努力。

structure.text: It
is a specialist predator of roe deer
a species that has exploded in Britain in recent decades
that has exploded in Britain in recent decades
holding back, by intensive browsing, attempts to re-establish forests

difficult_points: It 指 the lynx。
a species 是 roe deer 的同位语，后接定语从句说明其数量激增。
holding back 是现在分词结果状语，说明狍鹿数量激增带来的影响。
by intensive browsing 是插入的方式状语，说明阻碍森林恢复的原因。

sentences.sentence: It will also winkle out sika deer: an exotic species that is almost impossible for human beings to control, as it hides in impenetrable plantations of young trees.
sentences.translation: 它还会把梅花鹿从藏身处赶出来：梅花鹿是一种外来物种，几乎不可能由人类控制，因为它躲在难以穿透的幼树人工林中。

structure.text: It
will also winkle out sika deer
an exotic species that is almost impossible for human beings to control
that is almost impossible for human beings to control
as it hides in impenetrable plantations of young trees

difficult_points: It 仍指猞猁，说明猞猁对另一种鹿的作用。
winkle out 表示把躲藏的动物找出或赶出。
冒号后的 an exotic species 是对 sika deer 的解释。
as 引导原因状语从句，解释为什么人类难以控制这种鹿。

sentences.sentence: The attempt to reintroduce this predator marries well with the aim of bringing forests back to parts of our bare and barren uplands.
sentences.translation: 重新引入这种捕食者的尝试，与把森林带回我们光秃贫瘠高地部分地区的目标非常契合。

structure.text: The attempt to reintroduce this predator
to reintroduce this predator
marries well with the aim of bringing forests back to parts of our bare and barren uplands
of bringing forests back to parts of our bare and barren uplands

difficult_points: The attempt to reintroduce this predator 是主语，to reintroduce 说明 attempt 的内容。
marries well with 是比喻说法，表示两项目标契合。
of bringing forests back 修饰 the aim，说明目标的具体内容。
bare and barren uplands 强调高地缺少树木、生态贫瘠。

sentences.sentence: The lynx requires deep cover, and as such presents little risk to sheep and other livestock, which are supposed, as a condition of farm subsidies, to be kept out of the woods.
sentences.translation: 猞猁需要茂密的隐蔽环境，因此对羊和其他牲畜几乎没有风险；而这些牲畜按农场补贴条件本来就应被挡在林地之外。

structure.text: The lynx
requires deep cover
and as such presents little risk to sheep and other livestock
which are supposed, as a condition of farm subsidies, to be kept out of the woods
as a condition of farm subsidies

difficult_points: as such 表示“因此；以这种身份”，连接深林需求与低风险结论。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 sheep and other livestock。
are supposed to be kept out 是被动表达，说明牲畜按规定应被排除在林地之外。
as a condition of farm subsidies 是插入条件说明。

sentences.sentence: On a recent trip to the Cairngorm Mountains, I heard several conservationists suggest that the lynx could be reintroduced there within 20 years.
sentences.translation: 在最近一次前往凯恩戈姆山脉的旅行中，我听到几位自然保护人士提出，猞猁可以在20年内被重新引入那里。

structure.text: On a recent trip to the Cairngorm Mountains
I
heard several conservationists suggest
that the lynx could be reintroduced there within 20 years

difficult_points: On a recent trip 提供作者听到建议的场景。
heard several conservationists suggest 是感官动词结构，suggest 前省略 to。
that 从句说明保护人士建议的具体内容。
could be reintroduced 是被动语态，表示猞猁被人类重新引入。

expressions.meaning_zh: 在最近一次去……的旅行中

sentences.sentence: If trees return to the bare hills elsewhere in Britain, the big cats could soon follow.
sentences.translation: 如果树木重新回到英国其他地方光秃的山丘上，这些大型猫科动物可能很快也会随之而来。

structure.text: If trees return to the bare hills elsewhere in Britain
the big cats
could soon follow

difficult_points: If 从句提出猞猁回归的生态前提：森林恢复。
the big cats 指猞猁，而不是所有大型猫科动物。
follow 在这里表示在森林恢复之后随之回归。

sentences.sentence: There is nothing extraordinary about these proposals, seen from the perspective of anywhere else in Europe.
sentences.translation: 从欧洲其他任何地方的角度看，这些提议并没有什么特别之处。

structure.text: There is nothing extraordinary about these proposals
seen from the perspective of anywhere else in Europe
from the perspective of anywhere else in Europe

difficult_points: There is nothing extraordinary 表示这些提议并不反常或激进。
seen from... 是过去分词短语，说明判断角度。
anywhere else in Europe 与英国形成对比。

sentences.sentence: The lynx has now been reintroduced to the Jura Mountains, the Alps, the Vosges in eastern France and the Harz mountains in Germany, and has re-established itself in many more places.
sentences.translation: 猞猁如今已经被重新引入汝拉山脉、阿尔卑斯山、法国东部的孚日山脉以及德国的哈茨山脉，并且已经在更多地方重新建立了种群。

structure.text: The lynx
has now been reintroduced to the Jura Mountains, the Alps, the Vosges in eastern France and the Harz mountains in Germany
and has re-established itself in many more places
in many more places

difficult_points: has now been reintroduced 是现在完成时被动语态，强调重新引入已发生且与现在相关。
多个地名并列，说明欧洲已有许多重引入案例。
has re-established itself 表示猞猁不仅被放归，还重新形成稳定存在。
and 连接两个现在完成时谓语，共用主语 The lynx。

sentences.sentence: The European population has tripled since 1970 to roughly 10,000.
sentences.translation: 自1970年以来，欧洲的猞猁种群数量已经增长到原来的三倍，约为一万只。

structure.text: The European population
has tripled since 1970 to roughly 10,000
since 1970
to roughly 10,000

difficult_points: has tripled 使用现在完成时，表示从1970年到现在的增长结果。
The European population 指欧洲的猞猁种群。
to roughly 10,000 表示增长后的数量约为一万。

sentences.sentence: As with wolves, bears, beavers, boar, bison, moose and many other species, the lynx has been able to spread as farming has left the hills and people discover that it is more lucrative to protect charismatic wildlife than to hunt it, as tourists will pay for the chance to see it.
sentences.translation: 和狼、熊、海狸、野猪、野牛、驼鹿以及许多其他物种一样，随着农业离开山区、以及人们发现保护有魅力的野生动物比猎杀它更有利可图，猞猁得以扩散，因为游客会愿意付费获得观赏它的机会。

structure.text: As with wolves, bears, beavers, boar, bison, moose and many other species
the lynx
has been able to spread
as farming has left the hills and people discover that it is more lucrative to protect charismatic wildlife than to hunt it
that it is more lucrative to protect charismatic wildlife than to hunt it
as tourists will pay for the chance to see it

difficult_points: As with... 表示猞猁的情况与一系列其他物种类似。
as farming has left the hills 引导原因或背景状语从句，说明扩散发生的社会生态条件。
people discover that... 与 farming has left 并列，共同解释猞猁扩散的背景。
more lucrative to protect... than to hunt it 说明经济激励从猎杀转向保护。

sentences.sentence: Large-scale rewilding is happening almost everywhere - except Britain.
sentences.translation: 大规模再野化几乎正在各地发生——除了英国。

structure.text: Large-scale rewilding
is happening almost everywhere
except Britain

difficult_points: Large-scale 强调再野化并非零散小项目。
almost everywhere 与 except Britain 形成鲜明对比。
破折号后的 except Britain 突出英国落后于欧洲其他地区。

sentences.sentence: Here, attitudes are just beginning to change.
sentences.translation: 在英国，人们的态度才刚刚开始改变。

structure.text: Here
attitudes
are just beginning to change

difficult_points: Here 指英国，与上一句 except Britain 衔接。
just beginning 强调变化才刚开始，程度仍有限。
attitudes 指公众和保护界对再野化的态度。

sentences.sentence: Conservationists are starting to accept that the old preservation-jar model is failing, even on its own terms.
sentences.translation: 自然保护人士开始接受这样一个事实：旧的“保存罐”模式正在失败，即便按它自己的标准来看也是如此。

structure.text: Conservationists
are starting to accept
that the old preservation-jar model is failing
even on its own terms

difficult_points: are starting to accept 表示态度变化正在发生。
that 引导 accept 的宾语从句。
the old preservation-jar model 回指前文把自然像腌菜罐一样保存的保护模式。
even on its own terms 表示即使用这种模式自己的标准衡量，它也失败了。

sentences.sentence: Already, projects such as Trees for Life in the Highlands provide a hint of what might be coming.
sentences.translation: 像苏格兰高地的 Trees for Life 这样的项目已经显示出未来可能会发生什么。

structure.text: projects such as Trees for Life in the Highlands
such as Trees for Life in the Highlands
provide a hint of what might be coming
what might be coming

difficult_points: Already 表示迹象已经出现。
such as Trees for Life in the Highlands 举例说明项目类型。
what might be coming 是介词 of 的宾语从句，指未来可能的发展。
a hint of 表示初步迹象，而非完整结果。

sentences.sentence: An organisation is being set up that will seek to catalyse the rewilding of land and sea across Britain, its aim being to reintroduce that rarest of species to British ecosystems: hope.
sentences.translation: 一个组织正在成立，它将努力推动英国陆地和海洋的再野化；它的目标是把最稀有的那个“物种”重新引入英国生态系统：希望。

structure.text: An organisation
is being set up
that will seek to catalyse the rewilding of land and sea across Britain
to catalyse the rewilding of land and sea across Britain
its aim being to reintroduce that rarest of species to British ecosystems
hope

difficult_points: is being set up 是现在进行时被动语态，表示组织正在被建立。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 An organisation，说明该组织将做什么。
its aim being... 是独立主格结构，补充该组织的目标。
冒号后的 hope 是 that rarest of species 的解释，把希望比喻成需要重新引入的物种。
